mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signal robust labor demand and rising household incomes, which fuels consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 prices higher over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of PAYEMS monthly change on forward 20-day returns is less than 0.03 or the hit rate of positive returns when PAYEMS rises is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A clearly defined tightening cycle begins when the federal funds rate rises above 0.5% for the first time after a prolonged near-zero period, increasing borrowing costs and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Within 6 months of FEDFUNDS crossing above 0.5%, the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive more than 60% of the time, or the average return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and accommodative monetary policy (low FEDFUNDS) creates a powerful consumption-investment feedback loop, where rising consumer spending compounds with cheap credit to lift corporate profits and equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS growth is positive and FEDFUNDS is below 1%, the forward 20-day S&P 500 return distribution is not significantly higher than the unconditional distribution (Kolmogorov-Smirnov test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth has a stronger positive effect on equity prices when the inflation environment is benign (CPI inflation below 2% year-over-year), as earnings growth is not eroded by rising input costs or aggressive Fed tightening.
failure_condition: In periods when CPIAUCSL 12-month change is below 2%, the correlation between GDPC1 quarterly growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is not positive or is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls to very low levels (below 4.5%), the labor market becomes tight, signaling rising wage inflation and prompting the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, which depresses equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: In all episodes where UNRATE drops below 4.5%, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is non-negative or the proportion of negative returns is below 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong residential investment and consumer confidence, which leads to positive spillover effects on the broader economy and supports higher equity valuations over the subsequent three months.
failure_condition: The Spearman rank correlation between HOUST monthly change and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is less than 0.05 or not statistically significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) provide a comprehensive signal of a synchronized economic expansion, where manufacturing strength and consumer demand reinforce each other, lifting corporate profits and stock prices over the next month.
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS rise month-over-month, the median forward 20-day S&P 500 return is not positive or the proportion of positive returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: During the early phase of a monetary tightening cycle, rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) reinforces expectations of further rate hikes, increasing discount rates and lowering equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns over the next week.
regime_hint: early tightening cycle (first 12 months of rate increases)
failure_condition: Within the first 12 months of the first FEDFUNDS increase from near zero, higher CPIAUCSL monthly changes do not predict negative 5-day forward S&P 500 returns (sign test p > 0.10).
